name: kugl
documentation:
PyPI readme goes here.
    
    For documentation please visit the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/jonross/kugl).

modules.name: tests.test_pods
documentation: Tests for the pods table.

functions.name: tests.test_pods.test_missing_metadata
documentation: Verify pod.label() does not fail if metadata is missing

functions.name: tests.test_pods.test_name
documentation: Verify different locations for the pod name, including no name at all.

modules.name: tests.test_cache
documentation: Tests for data cache timeout behavior.

modules.name: tests.test_nodes
documentation: Tests for the nodes and taints tables.

modules.name: tests.test_cli
documentation: Tests for command-line options.

modules.name: tests.test_extra
documentation: Assorted query tests not covered elsewhere.

modules.name: tests.test_utils
documentation: More assorted tests, should these be combined with test_misc.py?

functions.name: tests.test_utils.test_jmespath_performance
documentation:
JMESPath performance regression test.  We use JMESPath to filter and transform
the data returned by the Kubernetes API.

modules.name: tests.test_misc
documentation:
Assorted tests for various edge cases and error conditions.
Some of these are just to achieve 100% coverage.

modules.name: tests.test_config
documentation: Tests for user configuration file content.

modules.name: tests.test_jobs
documentation: Tests for the jobs table.

modules.name: kugl.main
documentation: Command-line entry point.

modules.name: kugl.impl.registry
documentation:
Registry of resources and tables, independent of configuration file format.
This is Kugl's global state outside the SQLite database.

classes.name: kugl.impl.registry.Registry

methods.name: kugl.impl.registry.Registry.add_schema
documentation: Register a class to implement a schema; this is called by the @schema decorator.

methods.name: kugl.impl.registry.Registry.add_table
documentation: Register a class to define a table in Python; this is called by the @table decorator.

All known tables and resources.
There is one instance of this in any Kugl process.

classes.name: kugl.impl.registry.Schema

methods.name: kugl.impl.registry.Schema.augment
documentation: Apply the built-in and user configuration files for the schema, if present.

methods.name: kugl.impl.registry.Schema.table_builder
documentation: Return the Table builder subclass (see tables.py) for a table name.

methods.name: kugl.impl.registry.Schema.resources_used
documentation: Return the ResourceDefs used by the listed tables.

class_variables.name: kugl.impl.registry.Schema.impl
documentation: FIXME use type vars

Collection of tables and resource definitions.

Capture a schema definition from the @schema decorator, example:
    @schema("kubernetes")
Or, capture a schema definition in a user config file.
Or both.

classes.name: kugl.impl.registry.GenericSchemaImpl
documentation: get_schema auto-generates one of these when an undefined schema is referenced.

modules.name: kugl.builtins.kubernetes
documentation:
Built-in table definitions for Kubernetes.

NOTE: This is not a good example of how to write user-defined tables.
FIXME: Remove references to non-API imports.
FIXME: Don't use ArgumentParser in the API.

methods.name: kugl.builtins.kubernetes.KubernetesData.get_objects
documentation:
Fetch resources from Kubernetes using kubectl.

```
param kind

Kubernetes resource type e.g. "pods"

return

JSON as output by "kubectl get {kind} -o json"
```

methods.name: kugl.builtins.kubernetes.KubernetesData._pod_status_from_pod_list
documentation:
Convert the tabular output of 'kubectl get pods' to JSON.
\:return: a dict mapping "namespace/name" to status

classes.name: kugl.builtins.kubernetes.LabelsTable
documentation: Base class for all built-in label tables; subclasses need only define UID_FIELD.

modules.name: tests.testing
documentation: Common utilities for unit testing.

functions.name: tests.testing.kubectl_response
documentation:
Put a mock response for 'kubectl get \{kind} ...' into the mock responses folder,
to be found by an invocation of ./kubectl in a test.
\:param kind: e.g. "pods", "nodes, "jobs" etc
\:param output: A dict (will be JSON-serialized) or a string (will be trimmed)

functions.name: tests.testing.make_node
documentation:
Construct a Node dict from a generic chunk of node YAML that we can alter to simulate different
responses from the K8S API.
\:param name: Node name

functions.name: tests.testing.make_pod
documentation:
Construct a Pod dict from a generic chunk of pod YAML that we can alter to simulate different
responses from the K8S API.

```
param no_metadata

Pretend there is no metadata

param name_at_root

Put the object name at top level, not in the metadata

param no_name

Pretend there is no object name
```

functions.name: tests.testing.make_job
documentation:
Construct a Job dict from a generic chunk of pod YAML that we can alter to simulate different
responses from the K8S API.

```
param name

Job name

param active_count

If present, the number of active pods

param condition

If present, a condition tuple (type, status, reason)

param

pod: If present, a pod dict to be used as the template, returned from make_pod
```

functions.name: tests.testing.assert_query
documentation:
Run a query in the "nocontext" namespace and compare the result with expected output.
\:param sql: SQL query
\:param expected: Output as it would be shown at the CLI. This will be dedented so the

> caller can indent for neatness. Or, if a list, each item will be checked in order.

```
param all_ns

FIXME temporary hack until we get namespaces out of engine.py
```

classes.name: tests.testing.Taint
documentation: Helper class for creating taints in test nodes

classes.name: tests.testing.CGM
documentation: Helper class for creating CPU/GPU/Memory resources in test containers

classes.name: tests.testing.Container
documentation: Helper class for creating containers in test pods

modules.name: kugl.builtins.helpers
documentation: Wrappers to make JSON returned by kubectl easier to work with.

classes.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.Limits

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.Limits.__radd__
documentation: Needed to support sum() -- handles 0 as a starting value

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.Limits.extract
documentation:
Extract a Limits object from a dictionary, or return an empty one if the dictionary is None.

```
param obj

A dictionary with keys "cpu", "nvidia.com/gpu" and "memory"
```

A class to hold CPU, GPU and memory resources. This is called "Limits" although it's used for both requests
and limits, so as not to confuse "resources" with Kubernetes resources in general.

classes.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.ItemHelper

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.ItemHelper.__getitem__
documentation: Return a key from the object; no default, will error if not present

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.ItemHelper.name
documentation: Return the name of the object from the metadata, or none if unavailable.

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.ItemHelper.namespace
documentation: Return the name of the object from the metadata, or none if unavailable.

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.ItemHelper.label
documentation: Return one of the labels from the object, or None if it doesn't have that label.
Some common code for wrappers on JSON for pods, nodes et cetera.

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.PodHelper.containers
documentation: Return the containers in the pod, if any, else an empty list.

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.PodHelper.main
documentation:
Return the main container in the pod, if any, defined as the first container with a name
in MAIN_CONTAINERS.  If there are none of those, return the first one.

methods.name: kugl.builtins.helpers.JobHelper.containers
documentation: Return the containers in the job, if any, else an empty list.

modules.name: kugl.impl.tables
documentation:
This is separate from engine.py for maintainability.
SQLite tables are defined and populated here.

classes.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableDef
documentation:
Capture a table definition from the @table decorator, example:
    @table(schema="kubernetes", name="pods", resource="pods")

classes.name: kugl.impl.tables.Table

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.Table.__init__
documentation:
---
param extras: extra column definitions from user configs (not from Python-defined
  tables)
param name: the table name, e.g. "pods"
param resource: the Kubernetes resource type, e.g. "pods"
param schema: the SQL schema, e.g. "name TEXT, age INTEGER"
---

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.Table.build
documentation:
Create the table in SQLite and insert the data.

```
param db

the SqliteDb instance

param kube_data

the JSON data from 'kubectl get'
```
The engine-level representation of a table, independent of the config file format

classes.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromCode

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromCode.__init__
documentation:
---
param extender: 'an ExtendTable object from the extend: section of a user config file'
param table_def: a TableDef from the @table decorator
---

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromCode.make_rows
documentation: Delegate to the user-defined table implementation.
A table created from Python code, not from a user config file.

classes.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromConfig

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromConfig.__init__
documentation:
---
param creator: 'a CreateTable object from the create: section of a user config file'
param extender: 'an ExtendTable object from the extend: section of a user config file'
param name: the table name, e.g. "pods"
---

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromConfig.make_rows
documentation:
Itemize the data according to the configuration, but return empty rows; all the
columns will be added by Table.build.

methods.name: kugl.impl.tables.TableFromConfig._itemize
documentation:
Given a row_source like
  row_source:
    - items
    - spec.taints
Iterate through each level of the source spec, marking object parents, and generating
successive row values
A table created from a create: section in a user config file, rather than in Python

classes.name: kugl.impl.tables.RowContext
documentation:
Provide helpers to row-generating functions.

Primarily, the `.data` attribute holds the JSON data from 'kubectl get' or similar.
The `.set_parent` and `.get_parent` methods allow row-generating functions to track
parent objects as they iterate through nested data structures.

modules.name: kugl.api
documentation: Imports usable by user-defined tables in Python (once we have those.)

modules.name: kugl.impl.config
documentation: Pydantic models for configuration files.

functions.name: kugl.impl.config.parse_model
documentation:
Parse a dict into a model instance (typically a UserConfig).

```
return

A tuple of (parsed object, list of errors).  On success, the error list is None.
On failure, the parsed object is None.
```
FIXME use typevars

functions.name: kugl.impl.config.parse_file
documentation:
Parse a configuration file into a model instance, handling edge cases.

```
return

Same as parse_model.
```
FIXME use typevars

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.Settings
documentation: Holds the settings: entry from a user config file.

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.UserInit
documentation: The root model for init.yaml; holds the entire file content.

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.ColumnDef

methods.name: kugl.impl.config.ColumnDef.gen_extractor
documentation:
Generate the extract function for a column definition; given an object, it will
return a column value of the appropriate type.

methods.name: kugl.impl.config.ColumnDef.extract
documentation: Extract the column value from an object and convert to the correct type.

methods.name: kugl.impl.config.ColumnDef._extract_jmespath
documentation: Extract a value from an object using a JMESPath finder.

methods.name: kugl.impl.config.ColumnDef._extract_label
documentation: Extract a value from an object using a label.
Holds one entry from a columns: list in a user config file.

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.ExtendTable
documentation: Holds the extend: section from a user config file.

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.ResourceDef
documentation: Holds one entry from the resources: list in a user config file.

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.CreateTable
documentation: Holds the create: section from a user config file.

classes.name: kugl.impl.config.UserConfig
documentation: The root model for a user config file; holds the complete file content.

classes.name: kugl.util.age.Age

methods.name: kugl.util.age.Age.__new__
documentation:
Create a new Age object.  Parameters may be one of
- a string like "10s", "5m30s", "1h", "2d12h"
- an integer number of seconds
- kwargs to pass to timedelta

methods.name: kugl.util.age.Age.parse
documentation:
Convert a string like "10s", "5m30s", "1h", "2d12h" to Python timedelta dict, using suffixes to
mean s = seconds, m = minutes, h = hours, d = days.

methods.name: kugl.util.age.Age.render
documentation: Render the age as a string like "10s", "5m30s", "1h", "2d12h".

A specialization of timedelta that handles age strings like "10s", "5m30s", "1h", "2d12h".
Also, an Age is always non-negative.
FIXME: Don't subclass timedelta, it's breaking the constructor contract.

functions.name: kugl.util.size.parse_size
documentation:
Translate a string a la 10K, 5Mb, 3Gi to # of bytes.  Returns an int if the result
can be represented as an int, else a float.

functions.name: kugl.util.size.to_size
documentation:
Given a byte count, render it as a string in the most appropriate units, suffixed by KB, MB, GB, etc.
Larger sizes will use the appropriate unit.  The result may have a maximum of one digit after the
decimal point.  If iec is True, use IEC binary units (KiB, MiB, etc).

functions.name: kugl.util.size.parse_cpu
documentation: Translate a "number of CPUs" field like "2", "1.5", or "300m" to float.

modules.name: kugl.util.sqlite
documentation: Our entire interface to SQLite features is here.

methods.name: kugl.util.sqlite.SqliteDb.query
documentation:
Query boilerplate reducer.
:param sql str: SQL query
:param data list: Optional query args
:param one_row bool: If True, use cursor.fetchone() instead of fetchall()
:param named bool: If True, rows are namedtuples
:param names list: If an array, append column names to it

methods.name: kugl.util.sqlite.SqliteDb.execute
documentation:
Non-query boilerplate reducer.
:param sql str: SQL query
:param data list: Optional update args

modules.name: kugl.util.clock
documentation: A wrapper on the system clock that can be replaced for unit testing.

modules.name: kugl.util.misc
documentation: Assorted utility functions / classes with no obvious home.

functions.name: kugl.util.misc.run
documentation:
Invoke an external command, which may be a list or a string; in the latter case it will be
interpreted using bash -c.  Returns exit status, stdout and stderr.

functions.name: kugl.util.misc.debug
documentation:
Turn debugging on or off for a set of features.

```
param features

list of feature names, parsed from the --debug command line option;
"all" means everything.
```

functions.name: kugl.util.misc.debugging
documentation: Check if a feature is being debugged.

functions.name: kugl.util.misc.dprint
documentation: Print a debug message if the given feature is being debugged.

classes.name: kugl.util.misc.KPath
documentation: It would be nice if Path were smarter, so do that.

classes.name: kugl.util.misc.ConfigPath
documentation: Same as a KPath but adds debug statements

modules.name: kugl.impl.engine
documentation:
Process Kugl queries.
If you're looking for Kugl's "brain", you've found it.
See also tables.py

classes.name: kugl.impl.engine.Query

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.Query.sql_schemaless
documentation: Return the SQL query with schema hints removed.
A SQL query + query-related behaviors

classes.name: kugl.impl.engine.TableRef

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.TableRef.parse
documentation:
Parse a table reference of the form "pods" or "kubernetes.pods".
SQLite doesn't actually support schemas, so the schema is just a hint.
We replace the dot with an underscore to make it a valid table name.

class_variables.name: kugl.impl.engine.TableRef.schema_name
documentation: e.g. "kubernetes"

class_variables.name: kugl.impl.engine.TableRef.name
documentation: e.g. "pods"
A reference to a table in a query.

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.Engine.__init__
documentation:
---
param config: the parsed user settings file
param context_name: the Kubernetes context to use, e.g. "minikube", "research-cluster"
---

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.Engine.query_and_format
documentation: Execute a Kugl query and format the rsults for stdout.

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.Engine.query
documentation:
Execute a Kugl query but don't format the results.
\:return: a tuple of (rows, column names)

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.Engine._get_objects
documentation:
Called for a cache miss, go actually fetch resources.
Handle built-in resources here, and dispatch to schema implementation for those
that aren't.

classes.name: kugl.impl.engine.DataCache

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.DataCache.__init__
documentation:
---
param dir: |-
  root of the cache folder tree; paths are of the form
  \<kubernetes context>/\<namespace>.\<resource kind>.json
param timeout: age at which cached data is considered stale
---

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.DataCache.advise_refresh
documentation:
Determine which resources to use from cache or to refresh.

```
param namespace

the Kubernetes namespace to query, or ALL_NAMESPACE

param resources

the resource types to consider

param flag

the user-specified cache behavior

return

a tuple of (refreshable, max_age) where refreshable is the set of resources types
to update, and max_age is the maximum age of the resources that won't be updated.
```

methods.name: kugl.impl.engine.DataCache.age
documentation: The age of a file in seconds, relative to the current time, or None if it doesn't exist.

Manage the cached JSON data from Kubectl.
This is a separate class for ease of unit testing.
